Welcome to Mumbai's first portal for hiring freshers

Need college students for a short-term survey? A hospitality hire that's a quick learner? A data entry operator who'll actually show up? Our candidates span accounting, data entry, hospitality, sales, creative media, and more — freshers across Mumbai, ekdum eager to work and looking for their first step.

Our story

43
Candidates
33
Resumes Ready
10+
Sectors

Pehli Kamai is an initiative by Tiny Miracles, a B-Corp certified social enterprise based in Mumbai and Amsterdam. For over a decade, Tiny Miracles has worked alongside communities across the city — on health, on education, on savings, on livelihoods — with one belief holding it all together: people don't lack ability, they lack access.

Somewhere along the way, a pattern became impossible to ignore. The children we'd watched learn to read had become young adults. They'd finished their 10th, their 12th, some their graduation — often as first-generation learners, often while working part-time, often without a single person at home who could explain what a resume was or what "HR round" meant, but always with hopes, dreams, and ambition. They'd taught themselves computers on borrowed phones. They'd signed up for Tally courses, animation courses, spoken English classes — on their own, with their own saved money.

And then they hit a wall that no course could fix: nobody would give them a job.

So we built
the bridge ourselves

Why this hire makes sense

What we have understood is that companies in this city need freshers. Thousands of capable young people need jobs. On paper, that's a simple match. In practice, it isn't — and it's nobody's fault in particular. Hiring is rushed, resumes all look the same, and shortcuts creep in without anyone choosing them.

A candidate's English, location they are from, clothes, or board of education quietly gets read before their ability does. We've caught ourselves doing it too, and are now unlearning.

We learnt that where you're from is not a qualification and not a warning sign. An accent is not a skill. A pin code is not a work ethic. Talent was never the shortage in this city — introductions were.

Why this is a smarter hire for you

When you set all that bias aside, here's what's left: young people who have solved real problems under real constraints, who understand India's actual mass-market customer better than most campus hires ever will, who are more resourceful and eager to get the job done.

None of that fits on a résumé. It shows up on the job.

Is there sometimes a skills gap? Yes — and that's exactly what our training closes. Candidates on this portal go through career-readiness preparation: reading a job description, building an honest resume, interviewing, and knowing what day one at a workplace actually looks like.

That's what Pehli Kamai is: the introduction. Meet the candidates, and then finish the sentence "freshers these days—" however you like. We think you'll be surprised.

Before you add your profile
We want every profile to be genuine and ready to work. This takes about 5 minutes and helps employers trust our candidates.
1
Background — a few questions about you (no right/wrong answers)
2
English check — simple reading and workplace questions
3
Work readiness — basic workplace scenarios
4
Choose your track — Corporate or NGO / Social Sector
Step 1 of 4 — Your Background
Step 2 of 4 — English Check  ·  Question 1 of 2
Step 3 of 4 — Work Readiness  ·  Question 1 of 2
Step 4 of 4 — Choose Your Track
Which type of work interests you?
This helps us match you with the right employers.

Sales, Admin, Finance, Retail, BPO, IT, Customer Service, Logistics
